message:
revert(combinator): restore short-circuit poll behavior in SelectAll and SelectAllDrain

Revert the "poll all futures before returning" approach from commit
f6192589. Eager polling of all futures after finding a ready one causes
problems: it swallows panics from simultaneously-ready futures and
silently drops their results. The original short-circuit design is
correct — poll futures in order, return immediately on first Ready.

SelectAll: Restore early return on first Poll::Ready, removing the
winner accumulator pattern.

SelectAllDrain: Restore early return with swap_remove→remove (preserving
index semantics) instead of the multi-index filter approach. Unpolled
futures after the winner are returned as losers, which is safe because
the caller is responsible for polling them.

Update tests to match the restored semantics:
- test_select_all_does_not_eagerly_poll_all_futures: verify only the
  winning future is polled (counter == 1, not 3)
- SelectAllDrain simultaneous-ready tests: losers now include unpolled
  ready futures (len 2, not 0) since they were never polled
- Drain loser test: add one extra pending poll for the yield-once loser

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
